meta.note: Display-only policy audit. The dashboard's computed avoidance window is always the 11am-5pm union of the audited windows below; nothing here feeds the computation. Verbatim quotes and page numbers match the methods-page HAP source table.
meta.audited_scope: Four plans audited to primary sources, plus the national advisory line. 'Not audited' means not audited here - not that the state has no plan.

national.window_text: Avoid going out in the sun, especially between 12.00 noon and 3.00 p.m.
national.source_note: standard national do's-and-don'ts line, reproduced verbatim in the DDMA Delhi HAP 2024–25, p. 54

Odisha.window_text: The working hours for daily labourers need to be re-scheduled i.e. from 6 a.m. to 11 a.m. and 3.30 pm to 6 pm.
Odisha.source_note: accessed via a document mirror; the OSDMA PDF did not serve directly

Andhra Pradesh.plan: Andhra Pradesh — APSDMA Heat Wave Action Plan 2020
Andhra Pradesh.window_text: Encourage employers to shift outdoor workers schedules away from peak afternoon hours (12 – 4pm) during a heat alert.

Gujarat.level_note: a city plan: Ahmedabad's is India's first and flagship HAP; Gujarat cities outside Ahmedabad are shown this as the nearest audited plan
Gujarat.window_text: Change working hours of labourers. Encourage employers to shift outdoor workers' schedules away from peak afternoon hours (1pm – 5pm) during a heat alert.

Delhi.window_text: Encourage employers to shift outdoor workers' schedules away from peak afternoon hours (1pm–5pm) during Heat alert. Working Hours will be from 7:00am to 1:00pm, 4:00pm to 6:00pm.

cpr.title: How Is India Adapting to Heatwaves? An Assessment of Heat Action Plans With Insights for Transformative Climate Action (Centre for Policy Research, March 2023)
cpr.quote_legal: HAPs have weak legal foundations. None of the HAPs indicate the legal sources of their authority.
cpr.quote_funding: HAPs are underfunded. Only 11 of 37 HAPs discuss funding sources. Of these, eight asked implementing departments to self-allocate resources, indicating a serious funding constraint.
cpr.note: CPR's findings are aggregate across the 37 plans it reviewed (2016-2022); it does not publish per-plan flags, so none are shown per plan here.
